YOGA PROGRAMThis isn’t just yoga. This is breathing like a butterfly. Stretching like a cat. Roaring like a lion, then resting in the shade.
Mini Monstars Kids Yoga is a play-based incursion program designed for children aged 0–6 years. We use storytelling, imagination, and gentle movement to introduce the fundamentals of mindfulness, emotional regulation, and body awareness.

From nursery to kindergarten, every session is tailored to meet the developmental stage of your children; led by experienced instructors who understand how young bodies learn, move, and rest.

  A Journey, Not Just a Class.
We don’t teach poses. We teach presence.
Our incursions take children on an imaginative journey; through jungles, oceans, farms, and forests, using yoga as the vehicle. Along the way, children develop:

  Mindfulness
Simple breathing techniques and sensory awareness games
  Storytelling
Themed adventures where poses become characters
  Strength & Balance
Gentle poses that build core stability and coordination
  Self-Expression
Moving, creating, and leading poses in their own way
  Emotional Regulation
Recognising feelings and using breath to reset
  Connection
Partner poses and group circles that build trust
  Focus
Following sequences, listening cues, and sustained attention
  Rest
Guided relaxation, visualisation, and quiet time





MUSIC PROGRAMLooking for the best music incursions for early learning centres in Australia? Mini Monstars Music delivers research-backed, high-energy programs designed specifically for children aged 0–6 years. Our incursions go beyond traditional kindergarten music; blending singing, dancing, instrument play, and musical storytelling to spark imagination, creativity, and joy.

Why Mini Monstars is the First Choice in Early Childhood Music Incursions:
Mini Monstars is more than a music program, we are an early learning partner. Our sessions are intentionally designed to align with the Early Years Learning Framework (EYLF) and the Australian Early Development Census (AEDC) , reinforcing why we are the trusted choice for educators across Australia.
  Body Percussion & Rhythm Games – Build coordination and listening skills.
  Memory Songs & Listening Activities – Strengthen auditory processing and focus.
  Rhythm & Melody Instruments – Hands-on exploration of real instruments.
  Drama, Movement & Musical Storytelling – Inspire imagination and language development.
  Games, Dances & Music Appreciation – Foster joy, social connection, and cultural awareness.


SENSORY FUSION PROGRAM
 More Than Sensory Play.

Intentional Practice. Visible Outcomes.

Every feather, every chime, every gentle pose in Mini Monstars Sensory Fusion is thoughtfully designed to support the Early Years Learning Framework (EYLF), not as an afterthought, but as the foundation of our program.
We don't just create moments of wonder. We create moments that matter. Each sensory invitation is intentionally placed to nurture the whole child, building identity, connection, wellbeing, confident learning, and early communication. 

 Hello and goodbye songs create predictable rituals that build belonging, confidence, and emerging autonomy.
 Shared props and group movement teach cooperation, spatial awareness, and the joy of contributing.
 Gross motor play and sensory rhythm support physical strength, emotional regulation, and calm.
 Open-ended exploration of force, flow, and cause and effect fosters curiosity, persistence, and creative problem-solving.
 Musical cues, gesture, and movement invite children to communicate ideas and emotions before words arrive


Our incursions actively support EYLF Learning Outcomes, including:developing a strong sense of identity through self-expression and performance.connecting with others through group rhythm games and collaborative music-making.enhancing wellbeing and motor coordination through movement, dancing, and body percussion.building confident and involved learners by exploring instruments, memory songs, and musical storytelling.developing effective communication skills through listening activities, vocal play, and music appreciation.
We directly support the AEDC domains that set children up for lifelong success:Physical Health & Wellbeing: Gross and fine motor development through instrument play and dance.Social Competence: Turn-taking, group participation, and peer connection.Emotional Maturity: Building confidence, resilience, and self-regulation.Language & Cognitive Skills: Enhancing memory, auditory discrimination, and vocabulary through song.Communication & General Knowledge: Exploring musical concepts and cultural appreciation.

• **Communicate** retention timelines upfront with centres—confirm whether coaches are long-term or short-term assignments before program launch to manage expectations.
